content:
# Review

## Outcome

**Approved**

## Evidence

- All 94 milestone_record tests pass (0 failures, 0 ignored)
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes cleanly (no warnings)
- cargo fmt --all --check passes (no formatting issues)
- Amendment fr-12-5e41079a FIXED: find_matching_running_task_run (model.rs:435-438) now only matches exact run_id when the caller provides one — the old fallback to a sole legacy runless row (previously at lines 437-441) is removed. When run_id=Some('run-X') is passed, only entries with run_id=Some('run-X') match. Legacy runless entries are never silently adopted by named runs.
- Amendment fr-12-5e41079a VERIFIED in fs.rs: record_task_run_start (line 2815-2820) passes run_id to find_matching_running_task_run; when no exact match is found, lines 2852-2853 detect that a sole legacy open row exists and set allow_named_retry_after_legacy_open=true, allowing a NEW attempt to be appended alongside the stale legacy row instead of rewriting it.
- Amendment fr-12-5e41079a regression test: explicit_retry_after_legacy_open_appends_new_attempt (service.rs:2961-3049) seeds a legacy Running row with run_id=None, then starts a new run with run_id='run-2'; asserts find_runs_for_bead returns 2 entries (legacy + new), confirming both attempts are preserved in audit history.
- Amendment fr-12-5e41079a model test: find_matching_running_task_run_requires_exact_run_id_match (model.rs:880-904) verifies that a sole legacy runless Running entry does NOT match when the caller provides run_id='run-3'.
- Amendment fr-12-eaae97d7 FIXED: same_attempt (model.rs:307-313) no longer considers two terminal entries with the same started_at as the same attempt. The fallback path (line 310-311) now requires `(!left.outcome.is_terminal() || !right.outcome.is_terminal())`, so two terminal rows without run_id are never collapsed even if timestamps match.
- Amendment fr-12-eaae97d7 FIXED: collapse_task_run_attempts (model.rs:461-476) now delegates grouping to find_collapse_group_index which uses sequential pairing logic. For legacy entries without run_id, a terminal entry is only collapsed into a group via unique_open_legacy_group_index (model.rs:510-524), which requires the group to have (a) no existing terminal entry, (b) an open legacy entry with matching started_at. This pairs start/completion rows sequentially rather than by timestamp equality, so two separate legacy retries with identical timestamps remain as distinct attempts.
- Amendment fr-12-eaae97d7 model regression: collapse_task_run_attempts_preserves_same_timestamp_legacy_retries (model.rs:947-1001) seeds 4 legacy rows (Running+Failed+Running+Failed) all with same started_at, verifies collapse produces 2 entries with distinct outcome_details ('first retry' and 'second retry').
- Amendment fr-12-eaae97d7 integration regression: find_runs_for_bead_preserves_same_timestamp_legacy_retries (service.rs:1638-1718) writes raw NDJSON with same-timestamp legacy paired rows, verifies bead query returns 2 distinct collapsed entries.
- All prior acceptance criteria remain satisfied: milestone state answers 'what happened to bead X?', controller can update linkage after each run outcome, all new fields are optional for backward compatibility with existing NDJSON data

## Findings and Gaps

- Both final-review amendments (fr-12-5e41079a and fr-12-eaae97d7) have been addressed with code changes and regression tests in completion round 13.
- The outstanding risk — milestone mutations span separate lineage, snapshot, and journal files without a cross-file transaction — remains. All paths are serialized by the milestone mutation lock and replay-safe, making this an acceptable trade-off for file-based storage.
- AppError::RunStartFailed is still used (service.rs ~line 555) for rejecting non-terminal outcomes in update_task_run, which is semantically a finalization rejection. A more specific error variant would be cleaner but this is cosmetic, not a correctness issue — carried forward from prior reviews.
- The active_bead_ids() function in model.rs still contains its own internal latest_task_runs_per_bead() call while reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage computes latest runs separately. External callers of active_bead_ids would redundantly recompute, but this is minor and outside the scope of the current amendments.
- The allow_named_retry_after_legacy_open guard (fs.rs:2852-2853) correctly appends a new attempt alongside a stale legacy open row, but the stale legacy row itself remains in Running state indefinitely unless separately finalized. This is expected behavior — the old pre-upgrade row is preserved for audit rather than implicitly closed — but operators should be aware that legacy open rows may persist alongside newer completed attempts in lineage history.

## Follow-up and Amendments

None.
